chunk_summary: This section discusses the delivery ecosystems for pediatric micronutrient programs in India, focusing on governance escalation paths across Anganwadi, school, and health-system platforms, with emphasis on entitlement versus treatment differentiation and operational monitoring.
learning_objectives: Understand the three delivery ecosystems for pediatric micronutrient programs: Anganwadi, schools, and health-system touchpoints.
Differentiate between population-level prevention (e.g., prophylactic IFA, VAS) and clinical treatment (e.g., therapeutic iron, vitamin A).
Learn governance escalation paths for addressing operational issues in program delivery.
Analyze the role of fortification and FSSAI regulation in nutrition programs.
key_facts: Anganwadi/ICDS platforms deliver take-home rations (THR) or hot cooked meals (HCM) to registered children.
PM POSHAN integrates weekly IFA supplementation and biannual National Deworming Day (NDD) in schools.
Health-system touchpoints include VHND/VHSND, sub-centres, PHCs, and facilities for scheduled VAS, IFA, and deworming.
Governance escalation paths include AWW → supervisor/sector in-charge → CDPO for Anganwadi issues.
Prophylactic IFA should be paused during therapeutic iron treatment to avoid double dosing.
Fortification is regulated by FSSAI and integrated across all three ecosystems.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: ‘Mission Saksham Anganwadi and POSHAN 2.0 documents emphasize the importance of governance escalation paths for addressing operational issues in nutrition delivery.’
document_key_topics: PM POSHAN integration with IFA and NDD in schools
Health-system touchpoints for VAS, IFA, and deworming
Governance escalation paths for operational issues
